step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the whiteboard diagram and transcript: On-premises IBM Netezza database data is synchronized via AWS Storage Gateway and 2x10G Direct Connect into an S3 Landing Zone. Metadata and transformation pipelines are managed in RDS Postgres. Two stages of EC2 instances perform quality cleansing and transformations using the metadata scripts, moving data through a Transformation S3 bucket and then into Amazon Redshift. Redshift Spectrum processes data and exports it to a Publish S3 bucket, which finally transitions older data to S3 Glacier based on lifecycle policies.

graph.name: Netezza Migration to AWS Redshift
graph.notes: HIPAA-compliant ELT architecture migrating 20TB of historical data and 100GB daily increments from on-premises Netezza to AWS Redshift.

nodes.name: Netezza DB
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: On-premises database holding 20TB historical data.

nodes.name: Storage Gateway
nodes.notes: Handles daily incremental transfers (100GB/day) via NFS.

nodes.name: Landing S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Real-time landing zone with 2-day retention policy.

nodes.name: Quality Cleansing
nodes.notes: Runs quality check and cleansing scripts driven by RDS metadata.

nodes.name: RDS Postgres
nodes.notes: Stores ELT pipeline metadata and transformation scripts.

nodes.name: Transformation S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores cleansed intermediate data. 1-day retention.

nodes.name: Transformation
nodes.notes: Executes final data structuring and dimensions transformation.

nodes.name: Redshift
nodes.notes: Target data warehouse for analytics and ad-hoc Spectrum queries.

nodes.name: Publish S3
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Stores daily dashboards data. Retained for 1 year & 1 month.

nodes.name: Glacier
nodes.notes: Long-term archive storage for compliance and history.

edges.notes: NFS traffic sent over Direct Connect
Publishes reporting data via Redshift Spectrum
